claim_text: Although less routinely reported than the preceding IQ metrics, MSSIM, IWSSIM, FSIM, and HFEN are also used as IQ metrics for evaluating MR reconstructions.

sub_claims.text: chen2022 review supports that MSSIM/IWSSIM/FSIM/HFEN are alternative-but-less-common IQ metrics in MR reconstruction.

evidence.snippet: To compare the reconstructed images with the ground truth, most studies reported the structural similarity index measure (SSIM) and the peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) [see Fig.  4(b) ]. Fewer used NRMSE, MSE, and the signalto-noise ratio (SNR).
The most highly correlated quantitative metrics are high-frequency error norm (HFEN), MAE, PSNR, and MAE.

sub_claims.nuance: Of the four metrics named in the manuscript sentence, MSSIM/MS-SSIM, IW-SSIM, and FSIM do not appear in chen2022 at all. HFEN appears only in chen2022's inter-metric correlation/redundancy analysis, not in any 'less-routinely-reported alternative IQ metric' framing. chen2022's 'fewer used' tier is explicitly NRMSE/MSE/SNR — a disjoint set from the manuscript's list. Note: the stub claim text on file ('VIF, NQM, and HFEN') differs from the extractor's claim text ('MSSIM, IWSSIM, FSIM, and HFEN'); adjudication followed the extractor evidence. Either claim formulation lands UNSUPPORTED against chen2022 (VIF and NQM are also absent from chen2022).

remediation.suggested_edit: chen2022 does not enumerate MSSIM, IW-SSIM, or FSIM (nor VIF or NQM, per the alternate stub formulation) as MR-reconstruction IQ metrics; it lists 'fewer used' metrics as NRMSE/MSE/SNR and mentions HFEN only in a metric-correlation context. Either (a) cite a metrics-focused source that explicitly catalogs the named alternative MR IQ metrics, or (b) drop chen2022 from this sentence and re-cite per-metric to the primary sources that introduce or apply each metric in MR reconstruction. Also reconcile the stub vs. extractor claim-text discrepancy before re-grounding.

phrasings_tried: MSSIM | MS-SSIM | IWSSIM | IW-SSIM | FSIM | HFEN (case-insensitive regex across all chen2022 files)
alternative metric | less routinely (case-insensitive across content.txt and sections/)
multiscale | multi-scale | feature similarity | information-weighted | information weighted (case-insensitive across all chen2022 files)
Direct read of section 'C. Evaluation Metrics' end-to-end for any enumeration of less-common IQ metrics

section_checklist.section: IV. Meta-Analysis Method / A. Data Collection
V. Meta-Analysis Results / C. Evaluation Metrics

attestation.closest_adjacent: C. Evaluation Metrics, sentence: 'most studies reported the structural similarity index measure (SSIM) and the peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) [see Fig. 4(b)]. Fewer used NRMSE, MSE, and the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).' This is the only chen2022 passage that frames a 'less-routinely-reported' tier of IQ metrics, but its members (NRMSE, MSE, SNR) do not match the manuscript's list (MSSIM, IWSSIM, FSIM, HFEN). HFEN appears separately as a correlated-metrics note: 'The most highly correlated quantitative metrics are high-frequency error norm (HFEN), MAE, PSNR, and MAE.'
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: chen2022 does not credit another primary for an MSSIM/IW-SSIM/FSIM/HFEN catalog; the metrics simply are not enumerated as a group there.
attestation.out_of_context_check: Where HFEN does appear in chen2022, it is in a redundancy/correlation analysis among quantitative metrics, not in a usage-frequency characterization. Citing chen2022 for 'these are less-routinely-reported alternative IQ metrics' uses HFEN in a different context than chen2022's own.
